2026-04-24T16:26:17.6879654Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6880162Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6880564Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6880909Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6881198Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6881420Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6881585Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6881759Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6881922Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6882138Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6882326Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6882592Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-24T16:26:17.6882834Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). The closest existing issues ([#41946](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/41946), [#41650](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/41650), [open-telemetry/opentelemetry-dotnet-contrib#1730](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-dotnet-contrib/issues/1730)) describe `operation_Name` having the wrong value on `requests`, which is a different symptom.\\n\\nCould you clarify whether this is:\\n\\n- an issue in my setup\\n- `operation_Name` on `traces` being deprecated / out of scope going forward\\n- a bug in the .NET exporter\\u0026#39;s log mapping\\n\\nThanks,\\nAleksey\\n\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n`traces.operation_Name` is populated with ` /path`\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\n`traces.operation_Name` is empty\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nCreate a .NET 10 ASP.NET core application with the code below and run it using\\n\\n```\\n\\n  \\n    net10.0\\n    enable\\n    enable\\n  \\n  \\n    \\n  \\n\\n```\\n\\n```\\nusing System.Diagnostics;\\nusing Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore;\\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;\\nusing OpenTelemetry.Logs;\\n\\nvar builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);\\n\\nbuilder.Services.AddOpenTelemetry().UseAzureMonitor();\\n\\nbuilder.Services.Configure(o =\\u0026gt;\\n{\\n    o.IncludeScopes = true;\\n    o.IncludeFormattedMessage = true;\\n    o.ParseStateValues = true;\\n});\\n\\nbuilder.Services.AddControllers();\\n\\nvar app = builder.Build();\\napp.MapControllers();\\napp.Run();\\n\\n[ApiController]\\n[Route(\\u0026#34;[controller]/[action]\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic class PingController : ControllerBase\\n{\\n    private readonly ILogger _logger;\\n    public PingController(ILogger logger) =\\u0026gt; This behavior differs from the classic `AddApplicationInsightsTelemetry()` approach.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Details\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore 1.4.0`\n**Affected API:** `ILogger` → OpenTelemetry log exporter → Application Insights `traces` table (`operation_Name` field)\n**Scenarios:**\n- Using `AddOpenTelemetry().UseAzureMonitor()` on .NET 10 with ASP.NET Core — `traces.operation_Name` is empty while `requests.operation_Name` is populated correctly\n- Same request correlation (`operation_Id`) exists on both rows, so the activity context is available\n\n**Root ask:** Clarification on whether this is a bug, a known limitation/deprecation, or a setup issue; if a bug, a fix is requested\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe issue author provides a minimal reproduction — a .NET 10 ASP.NET Core app using `UseAzureMonitor()`, where an `ILogger.LogInformation` call inside a controller action produces a `traces` row with an empty `operation_Name`.\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1. Confirm whether the `operation_Name` mapping from `Activity.DisplayName` or `Activity.OperationName` is intentionally omitted in the log record processor/exporter path for `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore`\n2. Compare the log-to-telemetry mapping code in `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore` vs `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` to see if `operation_Name` population differs\n3. Check if `Activity.Current` is non-null at log time (the reproduction code logs it, so this can be verified) — if it is non-null but `operation_Name` is still empty, the mapping logic is the likely culprit\n4. Compare behavior with `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter` standalone to narrow down whether the issue is in the AspNetCore distro package specifically\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🏷️ Label Confidence\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Category:** `Client` — This is a data-plane SDK client library issue; the bug is in the .NET exporter/distro log mapping behavior, not an ARM/management or provisioning concern\n- **Service:** `Monitor - Distro` — Package `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore` lives under `/sdk/monitor/Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore/`, which is covered by the `%Monitor - Distro` service label\n- **Confidence:** High — Package is explicitly named in the issue title and body; category and service are unambiguous\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e👥 Owner Routing\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Monitor - Distro` (line 344) — sole label match; package path `/sdk/monitor/Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore/` is directly listed under this label\n- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed\n- **ServiceOwners:** @harsimar @mattsains-msft @rajkumar-rangaraj @xiang17\n- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` and `needs-team-attention` labels; posted @mention routing comment\n- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; line 1034 `%Monitor` (ARM/Mgmt Monitor) was encountered but requires \"Monitor\" not \"Monitor - Distro\" — skipped.
